# RSA Encryption in Ruby

Here's a complete example of RSA encryption implemented in Ruby:

```ruby
require 'openssl'

class RSAEncryption
  def initialize
    # Generate RSA key pair
    @key = OpenSSL::PKey::RSA.new(2048)
  end

  def encrypt(message)
    # Encrypt the message using public key
    encrypted = @key.public_encrypt(message, OpenSSL::PKey::RSA::PKCS1_OAEP_PADDING)
    Base64.encode64(encrypted)
  end

  def decrypt(encrypted_message)
    # Decrypt the message using private key
    encrypted_data = Base64.decode64(encrypted_message)
    @key.private_decrypt(encrypted_data, OpenSSL::PKey::RSA::PKCS1_OAEP_PADDING)
  end

  def public_key
    @key.public_key
  end

  def private_key
    @key
  end
end

# Example usage
puts "RSA Encryption Example"
puts "=" * 30

# Create RSA encryption instance
rsa = RSAEncryption.new

# Original message
original_message = "Hello, this is a secret message!"
puts "Original message: #{original_message}"

# Encrypt the message
encrypted_message = rsa.encrypt(original_message)
puts "Encrypted message: #{encrypted_message}"

# Decrypt the message
decrypted_message = rsa.decrypt(encrypted_message)
puts "Decrypted message: #{decrypted_message}"

# Verify the decryption worked
puts "Decryption successful: #{original_message == decrypted_message}"

# Display key information
puts "\nKey Information:"
puts "Public Key (PEM format):"
puts rsa.public_key.to_pem
puts "\nPrivate Key (PEM format):"
puts rsa.private_key.to_pem
```

## Output Example:
```
RSA Encryption Example
==============================
Original message: Hello, this is a secret message!
Encrypted message:
